title: 10L + GatedXSA + EMA + TrigramHash + QuadgramHash + SwiGLU (val_bpb=TBD)
body:
PS: I don't have funds for training on 8xh100.

Building on the 10L SOTA (2026-03-20_10L_Int5MLP_MuonWD04_SWA50, 1.14276 BPB). Four additions:

1. Gated XSA — XSA (arXiv:2603.09078) with a learned per-layer sigmoid gate instead of hard projection removal. Starts near full XSA (sigmoid(4.0) ≈ 0.98) and adapts during training. The intuition is that early and late layers may want different amounts of self-value suppression, so letting it learn seems better than fixing it at 1.0.

2. EMA — Exponential moving average (decay=0.997) replacing SWA. Shadow copy on GPU, updated every step. Feels cleaner than SWA since you don't have to pick a start fraction — it naturally tracks the model through the whole warmdown.

3. TrigramHash + QuadgramHash — Hash (t-2, t-1, t) and (t-3, t-2, t-1, t) into learned embedding tables (1024 buckets each, dim=64/32), added at the input. Cheap in terms of compressed bytes (~55KB and ~25KB respectively after int8+zstd) but gives the model explicit n-gram context at zero attention overhead. Uses int32 arithmetic for the hash to avoid overflow.

4. SwiGLU — Swapped relu² for SwiGLU with mlp_mult=2.0 (gate+up+down vs the old fc+proj). Param count stays the same as relu² at mlp_mult=3.0.

BigramHash is back to 10240 buckets (had to shrink it to 2048 in a previous 11L attempt to fit the budget — staying at 10L means we don't have to make that tradeoff).

All weights quantized int5 (MLP) / int6 (attn, bigram, trigram, quadgram proj) / int8 (embedding tables) + zstd-22. Fits within 16MB.
